$comment: Source of truth for an embedded Severity Calibration entry (Appendix B). One block per locked finding that was stress-tested for softening or over-escalation: it records the Triage-locked tier, the tier actually delivered in the letter body, the adjustment direction, and (for any change) the rationale. softness-check (honesty_check.py) reads `delivered` keyed by `id` from these blocks instead of parsing the appendix prose; structured-findings validates the blocks via the shared stdlib checker (required / const / enum / pattern). Author-facing docs (output-policy.md §Mandatory Appendices, findings-ledger-format.md) describe this block; they must not re-list the field set — this file is canonical. Closed-key (additionalProperties:false) enforced by the engine (Harness Contracts v2); declared closed in schemas/_coverage.json.
title: APODICTIC structured Severity Calibration entry (apodictic.severity_calibration.v1)

id.$comment: Finding Lifecycle ID this calibration entry is about (matches apodictic.finding.v1 `id`).

locked.$comment: The Triage-locked severity (the ledger's locked tier).

delivered.$comment: The severity actually delivered in the letter body. delivered below locked is a softening and requires a body softness-downgrade marker.

direction.$comment: Adjustment relative to the locked tier from the adversarial self-check.

rationale.$comment: One-sentence justification; expected whenever direction != unchanged.
